compartments.compartment: Summary: Implemented Quick Start presets and Dashboard integration: QuickStartDialog now includes 4 preset prompts and a create mode (app name + generate) that creates a schema and emits @add-to-canvas for Studio; the Dashboard header gains a Quick Start button that opens the dialog, pushes newly-created schemas into the store, and redirects the user into Studio. Also added header CSS (.header-actions, .header-btn), ensured Dashboard re-fetches schemas on activation, cleaned up Settings (dynamic provider lists, enable/disable toggles, collapsible groups, search, All/None buttons, badges/counters), suppressed Neo4j id() deprecation warnings via NotificationConfig, and fixed ResumeBanner to guard against empty schemaId (double-slash). TypeScript compiles clean and the changes are committed and pushed (commits: 0e759304, 47b4ea40).

facts.ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS: * QuickStartDialog provides preset prompts (4) and a create mode that generates a schema from the prompt and emits @add-to-canvas for Studio. * Dashboard header includes a Quick Start button that opens QuickStartDialog, pushes the created schema to the store, and redirects to Studio after creation. * Settings UI uses collapsible model groups, search, All/None controls, status badges, and counters for model management. * Studio re-fetches the active schema onActivated when returning to the Studio view.
facts.CONFIG_DEFAULTS: * quickstart.presets=4 * NotificationConfig suppresses Neo4j id() deprecation warnings.
facts.WORKFLOW_RULES: * When a schema is created from Quick Start, the Dashboard pushes it to the store and navigates the user into Studio.
facts.USER_DIRECTIVES: * Use the quick start prompt we used previously as a template for Quick Start entries. * Add Quick Start as an option on the Dashboard page alongside New App.

output.user_observations: * The user prefers UI templates and Quick Start shortcuts rather than typing prompts from scratch. * The user gives short, task-focused directives about UI behavior and placement.
